rulings.Case Title: RODRICK BRECKLER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES U.S.A., INC.'S Motion To Transfer Venue IS DENIED. VENUE IS PROPER IN THIS COUNTY AND DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MAKE THE REQUISITE SHOWING UNDER CCP SECTION 397(C). =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. ERIC AU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT EDWIN LAW MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary adjudication by Edwin Law directed to the second cause of action is granted for the reasons hereafter described. Each of the defendants shifted the burden of producing evidence based on their declarations. In response, plaintiff failed to present evidence that raised a triable issue of fact. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the complaint filed by the bankruptcy trustee for the contents of the complaint are inadmissible hearsay. The objections to the Samuels' declarations for lack of foundation are well-taken. There is an absence of facts providing a basis for this testimony and Mr. Samuels has not been qualified as a forsenic accounting expert. The Court does admit the testimony of the defendants given under oath as a hearsay exception and sustains the objection to all other documents attached to the Samuels' declaration as hearsay. The admitted testimony is not sufficient to create a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff has failed to provide a basis for a trier of fact to conclude that defendants controlled the arbitration directed against the corporate entity or may be deemed the alter eqo of the corporation and therefore personally responsible for representations made to plaintiff by the corporate entity or the business practices of the corporate defendant. Plaintiff's request for a further continuance for discovery has been denied. =(302/PJM/PB)

Plaintiff's request for a further continuance for discovery has been denied. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MARY WILLIAMS et al VS. JOHN PEITRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2009, line 4, PLAINTIFFS MARY WILLIAMS AND MICHAEL MCGUINNESS DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ALTHOUGH UNTIMELY UNDER CCP SECTION 430.40(b), THE COURT FINDS EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 19 AND 20. DEFENDANTS MUST IDENTIFY WHICH CAUSES OF ACTION THE DEFENSES ARE DIRECTED TO AND SET FORTH NEW FACTS THAT DEFENDANTS BASE THEIR CLAIM ON APART FROM WHAT IS IN THE COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: RAY BRONNER VS. RICHARD S KAIHARA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ray Bronner'S Amended Motion For Relief From Dismissal (Ccp 273), & Request For Order Shortening Time For Answering The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2009, line 15, Plaintiff Ray Bronner's Amended Motion For Relief From Dismissal (Ccp 273), & Request For Order Shortening Time For Answering The Complaint IS DENIED. THE DISMISSAL WAS ENTERED ON 5/29/08 AND PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED NO EXPLANATION FOR THE DELAY IN SEEKING TO SET IT ASIDE OR A BASIS FOR DOING SO. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. RAY BRONNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ray Bronner'S Motion For Calendaring Hearing On Sanctions (Ccp 128, 12.5, 128.6, 128.7)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2009, line 16, Defendant Ray Bronner's Motion For Calendaring Hearing On Sanctions (CCP 128, 12.5, 128.6, 128.7) IS DENIED. THIS IS AN IMPROPER MOTION. THE LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT DOES NOT SET CALENDAR DATES FOR A PARTY'S MOTIONS. IT IS UP TO THE PARTY TO NOTICE A MOTION FOR A PARTICULAR TIME AND DAY. IF A PARTY THEN SEEKS TO PRESENT ORAL TESTIMONY, A PARTY MAY MAKE THE REQUEST PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1306. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATIO VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Adn Return Of Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2009, line 8, RESPONDENT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS Motion To Set Aside Order And Return Of Sanctions. THE COURT HAS THE AUTHORITY TO RECONSIDER THE AWARD OF SANCTIONS GIVEN THE DECISION OF THE COURT OF APPEALS. VALDEZ V. HIMMELFARB (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 1261, 1265. THE SANCTIONS WERE AWARDED IN CONNECTION WITH THE STATE'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER, NOT IN CONNECTION WITH THE STATE'S INITIAL OPPOSITION. THE AWARDED SANCTIONS WERE APPROPRIATE BECAUSE THE STATE'S MOTION FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN UNDER CCP SECTION 1008. THE TRANSCRIPT IN THE RELATED CASE WAS AVAILABLE, THE ARGUMENTS ADVANCED WERE SUBSTANTIVELY IDENTICAL, AND THE LAW HAD NOT CHANGED. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: GAETANI REALTY, INC. VS. ADAM NEWTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To S/A Judgment Entered Pursuant To Stipulation; Memorandum Of P/A;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT CIRO MATARAZZO'S Motion To SET ASIDE Judgment Entered Pursuant To Stipulation IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO PRESENT FACTS TO SET ASIDE THE JUDGMENT ENTERED PURSUANT TO STIPULATION UNDER CCP SECTION 473. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE 12/16/08 STIPULATION WAS ENTERED INTO BY MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. =(302/PJM/VC)
